county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Cameron County formed 1848 from Nueces. County seat is Brownsville. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Brownsville. Rio Grande Valley — southernmost county in Texas. Brownsville Historical Association. Mexican-American War records (1846-1848). Fort Brown military records at NARA. Mexican land grant records at GLO. Significant Mexican-American heritage. Port of Brownsville records. Check Nueces for pre-1848 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Cameron County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1848; County Clerk in Brownsville holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Cameron County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1848 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Cameron County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1848. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Cameron County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1848. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Cameron County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Cameron County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Spanish Colonial / Mission Records -- Cameron County Area
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archives of San Antonio / TSLAC / Bexar Archives at UT-Austin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Spanish colonial records including mission registers (baptisms, marriages, burials), presidio records, and land grants. Cameron County area was part of the Spanish colonial system. Records at Catholic Archives of San Antonio, TSLAC, and Bexar Archives at UT-Austin.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Colonial-era records vary in survival; many originals at Catholic Archives or in Mexican archives

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Cameron County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Cameron County from 1848. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Camp County formed 1874 from Upshur. County seat is Pittsburg. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Pittsburg. Small East Texas county. Check Upshur County for pre-1874 records. Camp County Historical Commission. Agricultural and timber heritage. Northeast Texas African American community records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Camp County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1874; County Clerk in Pittsburg holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1874 to 1950 for Camp County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Camp County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Camp County from 1874. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Carson County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Panhandle. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Panhandle. Panhandle county organized 1902. Ranching heritage. Carson County Square House Museum. Check Bexar County for pre-organization records. Carson County was part of the open range cattle era.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Carson County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Panhandle holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Cass County formed 1846 from Bowie. County seat is Linden. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1860 courthouse fire destroyed records.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Linden. 1860 courthouse fire destroyed records — check TSLAC for backups. Northeast Texas county (originally Davis County, renamed 1871). Cass County had a large plantation economy. Significant African American history. Check Bowie for pre-1846 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Cass County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Linden holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Cass County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1860 to present. Some gaps due to 1860 courthouse fire destroyed records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1860. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1860. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Cass County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Cass County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Cass County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Cass County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Cass County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Cass County from 1846. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Castro County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Dimmitt. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Dimmitt. Panhandle county organized 1891. Castro County Historical Museum. Farming county (irrigation agriculture). Check Bexar County for pre-organization records. Named for Henri Castro, Alsatian empresario who colonized Medina County.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Castro County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Dimmitt holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Chambers County formed 1858 from Jefferson, Liberty. County seat is Anahuac. Gulf Coast region with port, plantation, and petrochemical heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Anahuac. Gulf Coast county. Battle of Anahuac (1832) was precursor to Texas Revolution. Chambers County Heritage Society. Check Jefferson and Liberty for pre-1858 records. Wallisville Heritage Park. Shrimping and fishing industry records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Chambers County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Anahuac holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Cherokee County formed 1846 from Nacogdoches. County seat is Rusk. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1847 fire; some early records lost.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Rusk. 1847 fire destroyed some early records. East Texas county with Caddo Indian heritage. Cherokee County Historical Commission. Cherokee were forced out in 1839 — check NARA for removal records. Iron industry in Rusk (19th century). Freedmen's Bureau records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Cherokee County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Rusk holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Childress County formed 1876 from Bexar, Fannin. County seat is Childress. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Childress. Rolling Plains county organized 1887. Childress County Heritage Museum. Railroad town (Fort Worth and Denver Railway). Check Bexar and Fannin for pre-organization records. Ranching heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Childress County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Childress holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Childress County Area (Comanche and Kiowa)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Comanche and Kiowa in the Childress County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Childress County from 1876. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Clay County formed 1857 from Cooke. County seat is Henrietta. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Henrietta. North Texas frontier county organized 1861. Clay County Heritage Museum. Red River region. Indian raids in 1860s-1870s era. Check Cooke County for pre-1857 records. Ranching and farming.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Clay County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1857; County Clerk in Henrietta holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Cochran County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Morton. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Morton. West Texas county organized 1924. Cochran County Historical Museum. Farming (cotton, grain). Check Bexar County for pre-organization records. One of the last Texas counties organized.
